name: Batman
description: The Dark Knight — Bruce Wayne, Gotham, gadgets, and the Rogues' Gallery.

questions.q: What is the real-world identity of Batman?

source.notes: Billionaire industrialist, Gotham City.

questions.q: In which fictional city does Batman operate?

source.notes: Often depicted as a New York analogue.

questions.q: Who is Batman's loyal butler and closest confidant?

source.notes: Raised Bruce after his parents' deaths.

questions.q: Which iconic beam of light is used to summon Batman to crime scenes?

source.notes: Projected by the GCPD under Commissioner Gordon.

questions.q: Which clown-faced criminal mastermind is Batman's archenemy?

source.notes: First appeared in Batman #1, 1940.

questions.q: Who is Batman's cat-burglar love interest, known as a skilled thief?

source.notes: Alter ego: Selina Kyle.

questions.q: What is the real name of Batman's first teen sidekick, Robin?

source.notes: Originally a circus acrobat, later became Nightwing.

questions.q: Which Gotham police officer is Batman's primary ally within the law?

source.notes: Father of Barbara Gordon (Batgirl).

questions.q: What were the first names of Bruce Wayne's murdered parents?

source.notes: Killed by Joe Chill in Crime Alley.

questions.q: Which Batman villain is an eco-terrorist whose powers come from toxins and plants?

source.notes: Dr. Pamela Isley.

questions.q: Harley Quinn first appeared in which 1990s animated series?

source.notes: Introduced in the 1992 animated episode 'Joker's Favor.'

questions.q: Which villain famously broke Batman's back in the 1993 'Knightfall' arc?

source.notes: Played by Tom Hardy in The Dark Knight Rises.

questions.q: Which Batman villain is a former prosecutor whose face was disfigured with acid?

source.notes: Harvey Dent, a former Gotham District Attorney.

questions.q: Which umbrella-wielding Gotham villain is also an aristocratic socialite?

source.notes: Oswald Cobblepot.

questions.q: Which Batman villain is a psychiatrist whose toxins induce paralyzing fear?

source.notes: Dr. Jonathan Crane, played by Cillian Murphy in Nolan's trilogy.

questions.q: In the comics, Bruce Wayne's late-father company, which bankrolls the Bat-gadgets, is called what?

source.notes: Lucius Fox often runs it for Bruce.

questions.q: Who trained Bruce Wayne in martial arts and later leads the shadowy League of Assassins?

source.notes: 'Demon's Head.' Liam Neeson in Batman Begins.

questions.q: Talia al Ghul, Ra's al Ghul's daughter, mothers which of Batman's sons?

source.notes: Damian debuted as Batman's biological son in 2006.

questions.q: Who co-created Batman in 1939 alongside officially credited Bob Kane?

source.notes: Finger finally received official co-creator credit in 2015.

questions.q: Batman first appeared in which issue of Detective Comics?

source.notes: May 1939 — the 'DC' in DC Comics.

questions.q: Which voice actor played Batman in Batman: The Animated Series (1992) and many DC animated projects thereafter?

source.notes: Conroy died in 2022.

questions.q: In what city is Arkham Asylum — Gotham's most famous mental hospital for villains — located?

source.notes: Named for Amadeus Arkham, its founder in comics lore.
